old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No animal-husbandry dependency remains because the scoped preserved cloth is made from tree-bast plant fibers rather than wool or another animal product.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract reports that the woven fibers are locally sourced oak bast and revises earlier flax and wool identifications.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Microscopy establishes a plant-bast raw material for the preserved woven textiles rather than managed-animal fiber.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Animal husbandry may enable later wool weaving, but it is unrelated to the material requirements of this evidence-locked node.

ontology_before: All textile weaving was assumed to require managed animals as a fiber source.
ontology_after: The Catalhoyuk weaving node follows its directly identified plant material without a wool dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: textiles_weaving no longer directly depends on animal_husbandry.
would_reject_if: The scoped Catalhoyuk samples were reidentified as animal fiber rather than tree bast.
short_reason: The analyzed textiles are plant bast, not animal fiber.
